methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Summit Nanotech is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Amanda Hall that has raised approximately C$120M to date. The valuation universe consists of nine peers within Climate & Energy—one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen—though direct same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables are limited.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable in this instance. The Climate & Energy sector peer set lacks sufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier datapoints (zero matches across each filter) to support a reliable median multiple. An all-sector median approach would conflate materially different risk profiles and capital requirements across the broader Climate & Energy landscape and therefore would be misleading. The fragmented stage distribution and heterogeneous business models among the nine identified peers prevent triangulation via this method.

Given the inapplicability of peer multiples, a credible valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. Additional methodologies—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture capital method calibration, or expansion of the comparable set to include adjacent sectors or later-stage rounds—would be required to establish a defensible valuation. Key uncertainties include the absence of financial performance data for Summit Nanotech and the lack of transaction multiples from recent Series A closings in the nanotech subsector.
